article_title: Astronomers Report First Robust Measurement of Magnetism on Exoplanets, Inferred From Strange Winds on Seven Hot Jupiters

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Astronomers Report First Robust Measurement of Magnetism on Exoplanets, Inferred From Strange Winds on Seven Hot Jupiters
content_preview.summary: An ESO and Gemini North team found winds on seven hot Jupiters slow as the planets get hotter, a pattern best explained by planet-wide magnetic fields.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Astronomers have reported what they describe as the first robust measurement of magnetism on planets outside the Solar System, based on the behavior of winds on a sample of scorching gas giants. According to the [European Southern Observatory](https://www.eso.org/public/news/eso2606/), the observations revealed that the winds on these planets are most likely governed by magnetic fields, providing the first robust measurement of magnetism on planets outside the Solar System. The resu...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-paced astronomy News piece (600 words) that explains an indirect, non-trivial result (winds as a magnetism proxy) and is careful to flag the inference as indirect.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200; Phys.org via Archive.org fallback, content intact). source-0 (ESO) confirms the seven hot Jupiters, the 'first robust measurement of magnetism' framing, the wind-speed figures (7200 km/h to over 25 000 km/h, vs ~1500 km/h for Jupiter — the article normalizes the separators to 7,200 / 25,000 / 1,500), the hotter-the-planet-slower-the-wind pattern, the magnetic-field 'brake', the Julia Seidel and Bibiana Prinoth quotes, and the ELT outlook. source-1 (Space.com) confirms the mph conversions (4,470 / 15,530 / 930), the Nature Astronomy June 2 publication, and the infer-the-strength framing. source-2 (Phys.org) confirms the Vivien Parmentier 'totally counter-intuitive…' quote verbatim and the ESPRESSO / Gemini North / Atacama instrument details.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote is verbatim and correctly attributed; every wind-speed figure matches the source (thousands-separator normalization only). The paywalled Nature Astronomy DOI was correctly excluded since its publication facts are carried by the accessible outlets. No fabricated specifics; the seven planets are appropriately left unnamed (as the sources do).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' correctly emphasizes the indirect nature of the measurement.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct from the recently-covered JWST Saturn and Long March / Blue Origin space stories.
